content: The __GFP_HIGHMEM flag can't be used with kmalloc and friends since the memory might not be in the kernel's address space. However, I need it for Page, where this is not an issue. Do we want to just add it to Flags, or do we want a separate HighmemFlags so that you can't accidentally pass __GFP_HIGHMEM to kmalloc?
I think it makes a lot of sense to prevent people from using flags that are incompatible/don't do anything
We'll need a bunch of duplication, though.
Could we have an "unsafe" flags type that does all the low level operations and then we just create newtype wrappers. Those then only expose the correct constants
For flags that work in both places.
though maybe we shouldn't name them all Flags...
Also, there's the question of whether this is a soundness issue. What does kmalloc do if you pass it GFP_HIGHMEM?
I think even if it is not an issue, we should do it.
we have a nice type system and we should use it.
I don't really want to bug down my uaccess patch series with several versions just for discussing this issue. Perhaps I should send the flag change separately ...
Yeah, if I base the flag addition on top of the uaccess patch series, then it can evolve independently. I'll do that.
